macro_summary: Markets face a catalyst-dense week anchored by the June CPI print and Fed Chair Warsh congressional testimony on July 14, followed by TSMC earnings July 16 and a dense late-July earnings wave (MSFT Jul 29, AAPL Jul 30). The Fed holds rates at 3.50–3.75% with core PCE elevated near 3.3%; futures price only ~15-23bps of additional tightening, but a hot CPI could sharply reprice hike odds ahead of the July 28-29 FOMC. AI capex confirmation from hyperscaler prints is the dominant equity theme, with the semiconductor complex still 11% below June peaks offering a compressed entry window into structurally intact demand.
screening_note: 22 overnight screening runs analyzed; minimum qualifying threshold set at 2 appearances. 7 tickers qualified. Ideas ranked by appearance count descending.
key_events: Jul 13: SKHY begins regular trading (ticker converts from SKHYV)
Jul 14: June CPI release 8:30 ET — binary macro event
Jul 14: Fed Chair Warsh congressional testimony
Jul 14: Major bank earnings — JPM, BAC, GS, WFC, C
Jul 16: TSMC Q2 2026 earnings — sector-wide semi re-rating event
Jul 28-29: FOMC meeting (~25% hike probability priced)
Jul 29: MSFT Q4 FY2026 earnings + FY2027 capex guidance

ideas.name: Microsoft Corporation
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 21 of 22 overnight screens with unanimous high-conviction votes, making it the strongest consensus idea in this digest. MSFT sits at its cheapest forward multiple since 2023 (~19-22x NTM EPS) despite 40% Azure growth and a $37B AI revenue run rate, while 97 analysts hold a consensus price target of ~$589-592 vs. a current price near $382-383 — implying ~54% upside. The July 29 Q4 FY2026 earnings print will also deliver FY2027 AI capex guidance, a direct confirmation catalyst for the broader AI trade. Defined-risk August call spreads are the preferred structure to capture a potential 5-10% earnings re-rating while limiting downside if guidance disappoints or macro turns hawkish.
ideas.catalyst: Q4 FY2026 earnings July 29, 2026; FY2027 capex guidance on same call; June CPI July 14 as binary valuation macro setup
ideas.edge: Analyst consensus target ~$589-592 vs. ~$382 current = ~54% implied upside; trading at ~19-22x NTM forward EPS, cheapest since 2023, despite 40% Azure growth and $37B AI run rate; stock down 20-25% YTD
ideas.bull_case: A strong Azure acceleration print on July 29 combined with robust FY2027 capex guidance re-rates the stock toward its $589 consensus target; soft June CPI on July 14 removes the last hike overhang and accelerates multiple expansion.
ideas.bear_case: If Azure growth decelerates or FY2027 capex guidance disappoints, the stock could sell off despite cheap multiples; a hawkish CPI surprise on July 14 or a July FOMC hike would compress Nasdaq multiples further and weigh on the position.

ideas.name: SK Hynix Inc. (ADR)
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 21 of 22 overnight screens, making it a near-unanimous consensus idea alongside MSFT. SK Hynix's US ADR converts from ticker SKHYV to SKHY today (July 13), beginning regular trading with massive IPO momentum — priced at $149, closed day-1 at ~$168 (+13%) on 7x oversubscribed demand — backed by the largest foreign company US listing ever at $26.5B. The company dominates HBM memory supply, which is sold out through most of 2027, with revenue +41% YoY and net income +108% YoY; 35 of 37 covering analysts rate it Buy. Stock entry on any post-IPO consolidation is preferred over options given that the options market is immature in week one of regular trading.
ideas.catalyst: Permanent ticker SKHY begins regular trading July 13, 2026; Q2 2026 earnings July 29 expected to show revenue roughly doubling QoQ; TSMC earnings July 16 as near-term sector re-rating read-through
ideas.edge: IPO priced at $149, day-1 close ~$168 (+13%) on 7x oversubscribed demand; revenue +41% YoY, net income +108% YoY, EPS +104% YoY; HBM supply sold out through most of 2027; valuation discount vs. US-listed peer Micron not yet closed; CEO says memory shortage may last past 2030
ideas.bull_case: Structural HBM scarcity lasting through 2027+ and dominant market position support continued multiple expansion as US institutional investors gain index-eligible access; a strong TSMC July 16 print re-rates the entire sector and lifts SKHY ahead of its own July 29 earnings.
ideas.bear_case: IPO euphoria fades and post-lockup selling pressure emerges; options market illiquidity limits hedging ability in early weeks; geopolitical risk between Korea/US/China could disrupt supply chains or investor sentiment; macro rate shock on July 14 CPI compresses growth multiples.

ideas.name: NVIDIA Corporation
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 20 of 22 overnight screens with 16 high-conviction votes, NVDA presents one of the most extreme valuation dislocations in this digest relative to its own history. At a forward P/E of ~21.7x versus its 5-year average of 72x (per Goldman Sachs), NVDA trades as though AI demand is collapsing — yet data center revenue hit a record $75.2B last quarter with 85% YoY growth, HBM supply is sold out through 2027, and an $80B buyback is active. TSMC's July 16 earnings serve as the nearest hard catalyst for AI supply-chain confirmation, with NVDA's own Q2 FY2027 print due late August. Defined-risk August call spreads are preferred over outright stock to manage binary event risk.
ideas.catalyst: TSMC Q2 earnings July 16, 2026 (supply-chain read-through); hyperscaler earnings wave MSFT Jul 29, GOOGL/META/AMZN late July (AI capex confirmation); NVDA Q2 FY2027 earnings late August 2026
ideas.edge: Forward P/E ~21.7x vs. 5-year avg of 72x; 61 analysts avg 12-month target $301.62 vs. ~$210 current = ~43% implied upside; stock -17% from May peak; $80B buyback active; SOX 11% below June peak = compressed sector entry
ideas.bull_case: TSMC beats on July 16 and hyperscaler capex commentary confirms AI infrastructure acceleration through 2027, driving NVDA toward analyst consensus near $300 as the valuation gap to historical norms begins to close.
ideas.bear_case: Any softness in hyperscaler AI capex commentary or a July rate hike shock compresses growth multiples further; export control escalation with China could reduce addressable market and weigh on forward estimates despite strong current demand.

ideas.name: Apple Inc.
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 17 of 22 overnight screens, AAPL brings a rare combination of near-term momentum and a hard earnings catalyst. The stock is up 8%+ in July alone, approaching NVDA in market cap at ~$4.5T, after fiscal Q2 showed 17% revenue growth with iPhone sales +22% and Services at an all-time high $31B quarterly run rate. An 8-consecutive-quarter EPS beat streak and foldable iPhone buzz into the July 30 fiscal Q3 print create a strong pre-earnings setup. However, the 37x P/E is rich, and a defined-risk bull call spread (e.g., August 1 expiry) is preferred to capture the catalyst while limiting exposure to China/tariff and valuation compression risk.
ideas.catalyst: Fiscal Q3 FY2026 earnings July 30, 2026; guidance called for 14-17% revenue growth; foldable iPhone rumored for fall launch adding pre-earnings buzz
ideas.edge: 8 consecutive EPS beats; fiscal Q2 revenue +17% YoY to $111.2B, iPhone +22%; Services at all-time high $31B/quarter; stock up 8%+ in July alone; $100B buyback active; consensus target ~$315 vs. ~$284-308 current range
ideas.bull_case: A third straight double-digit revenue growth quarter combined with foldable iPhone pre-launch excitement and strong Services margin expansion could push AAPL past analyst consensus targets and further close the gap with NVDA for most-valuable company status.
ideas.bear_case: At 37x P/E, any miss on iPhone guidance or China revenue softness due to tariff disruption could trigger a sharp de-rating; the stock's 8%+ July run may already be pricing in a beat, limiting upside surprise room.

ideas.name: Invesco QQQ Trust
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 6 of 22 runs as a macro-driven options vehicle rather than a directional stock bet. July 14 delivers a simultaneous triple catalyst — June CPI at 8:30 ET, Fed Chair Warsh congressional testimony, and major bank earnings (JPM, BAC, GS, WFC, C) — creating one of the densest macro data days of the year for the Nasdaq-100. Futures price only ~15-23% probability of a July hike, but if CPI surprises hot, that probability reprices sharply with record margin debt of $1.42T (+54% YoY) amplifying any downside. A defined-risk QQQ bull call spread captures a soft CPI scenario; a put spread hedges the hawkish tail.
ideas.catalyst: June CPI release July 14, 2026 at 8:30 ET; Fed Chair Warsh congressional testimony July 14; FOMC meeting July 28-29 (~25% hike probability)
ideas.edge: Rates market pricing only ~15-23% July hike probability; weak June jobs (57K vs. 115K expected) tilts toward hold; soft CPI removes last hike fear and could unlock Nasdaq upside; Nasdaq up ~11-12% YTD with deep, liquid options market for defined-risk structures
ideas.bull_case: A soft June CPI print eliminates residual rate-hike fear, Warsh signals patience, and QQQ extends its 2026 rally heading into the MSFT/AAPL earnings wave — bull call spread captures this scenario with defined downside.
ideas.bear_case: A hot CPI print (May ran 4.2% YoY headline) forces markets to reprice July hike odds sharply higher; record margin debt of $1.42T amplifies forced selling in tech; put spreads on QQQ provide a cheap portfolio hedge against this tail.

ideas.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 5 of 22 overnight screens with 4 high-conviction votes, TSMC's July 16 Q2 2026 earnings report is the single highest-impact near-term event for the entire semiconductor sector this week. As the sole foundry for leading-edge AI chips serving NVDA, AAPL, and AMD, a TSMC beat simultaneously validates the NVDA, SKHY, and broader AI capex theses. Q2 revenue guided at $39-40.2B (+32% YoY), Polymarket assigns 94.5% probability of a beat, monthly revenue filings have already de-risked the headline print, and analyst consensus holds 17 buys vs. 0 sells with a ~$520 average target implying 17-19% upside from ~$432. Bull call spreads expiring end of July are the preferred structure.
ideas.catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings report July 16, 2026 — 5 days away; Citi placed a 30-day upside catalyst watch pre-earnings; semiconductor Q2 earnings growth projected +131% YoY per FactSet
ideas.edge: Polymarket 94.5% beat probability; avg analyst target ~$520 vs. ~$432 current = ~17-19% upside; 17 buys, 0 sells analyst consensus; Q2 revenue guided +32% YoY; SOX 11% below June peak = compressed sector entry; monthly revenue filings already de-risk the print
ideas.bull_case: A beat-and-raise on AI wafer demand July 16 re-rates the entire SOX off its 11% discount to June highs and acts as a simultaneous bullish read-through for NVDA, SKHY, and ASML — Citi's upside catalyst watch captures the post-earnings momentum.
ideas.bear_case: Taiwan geopolitical risk (China-Taiwan tensions) is a persistent overhang that cap multiples regardless of fundamentals; any softer-than-expected AI wafer demand commentary could disappoint given elevated expectations and send semis lower across the board.

ideas.name: SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust
ideas.thesis: Appeared in just 2 of 22 runs — the minimum qualifying threshold — making this the lowest-conviction idea in the digest and best treated as a portfolio hedge rather than a directional bet. The June CPI print on July 14 creates a binary macro event for the broad market: a soft print with S&P up ~11% YTD and historically positive H2 follow-through (89% of similar years positive, avg +8.2%) could extend the rally; a hot print with ~25% July hike probability reprices risk sharply. A defined-risk SPY put spread into July 14 offers cheap tail protection given complacency, while a straddle captures two-way binary risk without a directional bet.
ideas.catalyst: June CPI release July 14, 2026 at 8:30 ET; FOMC meeting July 28-29 with ~25% hike probability priced
ideas.edge: S&P 500 up ~11% YTD; 89% of similar H1-strong years show positive H2, avg +8.2%; but ~25% July hike probability is an unpriced tail risk — put spread or straddle offers asymmetric hedge with defined cost into a binary catalyst in 2 trading days
ideas.bull_case: Soft June CPI eliminates residual July hike risk, Warsh signals patience on July 14, and the S&P continues historically strong post-H1 momentum into a dense earnings season with strong corporate fundamentals.
ideas.bear_case: A hot CPI print forces sharp repricing of July 28-29 FOMC hike odds; May CPI already ran 4.2% YoY headline, and if June reaccelerates, SPY faces a meaningful correction from already elevated levels.
